Step back in time!  Yesterday, we spent time at San Jose History Park and absolutely loved it. From the fantastic 1960s music poster exhibit to the charming historic buildings spread across 14 beautiful acres, there’s so much to explore and enjoy. A wonderful mix of history, culture, and nostalgia—definitely worth some visits and they have a lot of special events too! We'll be back! Highly recommmended.

History Park in San Jose offers a well-preserved collection of restored landmarks and replica buildings, providing a glimpse into the city’s rich heritage. Visitors can explore historical homes, museums, and interactive exhibits, including the Peralta Adobe-Fallon House and the Portuguese Heritage Museum, which showcase local history and cultural influences.

One of the most engaging annual events here is Scout-O-Rama, a large-scale scouting festival featuring hands-on activities for all ages. Families can participate in archery, water rockets, and pinewood derby races, while enjoying food trucks and live demonstrations. The event is open to the public, but parking can be challenging on busy days, so arriving early is recommended.
Indoor/outdoor museum modeled after a small early 1900s US town. Great for visitors of all ages.  Experience history through preserved buildings and exhibits.
